title: Graph Inequalities
why: To graph an inequality on a number line, you first need solid number line basics — knowing where numbers sit and which way is bigger. Warm up the number line and comparing numbers, and graphing inequalities is just shading the right direction.

diagnostic.q: On a number line, which number is farther right?
Numbers on a number line get bigger as you move…

learn.heading: The number line
learn.intro: On a number line, numbers go in order from left to right. Numbers get bigger to the right and smaller to the left. To graph n > 3, you shade all the numbers bigger than 3 — to the right.
examples: On the line … 0  1  2  3  4  5 … the number 5 is to the right of 2, so 5 > 2.
"Numbers greater than 4" live to the right of 4. "Numbers less than 4" live to the left of 4.

items.q: A. On the line 0 1 2 3 4 5, what number is just to the right of 3?
B. What number is just to the left of 3?
C. Which number is bigger: 1 or 6?
items.hint: 6 is farther right on the number line, so 6 is bigger.

items.q: A. To graph n > 5, you shade numbers…
items.hint: Greater than means bigger, and bigger numbers are to the right.

items.q: B. To graph n < 2, you shade numbers…
items.hint: Less than means smaller, and smaller numbers are to the left.

tiers.intro: Is the number part of the graph?

items.q: A. Is 6 part of the graph of n > 4?
B. Is 4 part of the graph of n < 4?
items.hint: n < 4 means strictly smaller than 4, and 4 is not smaller than itself.

exit.hint: Greater than = bigger = right side of the line.

exit.q: 2. Is 1 part of the graph of n < 3?
